steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. The passage introduces one Highwaymen painting as typical of the group, then makes the central claim: art historians suggest these paintings shaped popular perceptions of Florida that persist today, with their imagery now seen as classically Floridian.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To describe the historical circumstances in which paintings by the Florida Highwaymen experienced a resurgence in popularity.: the passage never mentions a resurgence in popularity; it discusses influence on perception, not a revival. ✗
  • Choice B — To explain that a particular painting by Roy McLendon has had greater influence on the broader culture of the state of Florida than is generally acknowledged.: McLendon's painting is only one example of the group's typical style; the argument is about the Highwaymen collectively, not McLendon specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — To contrast the public's reaction during the 1950s and '60s to a particular painting by Roy McLendon with more recent reactions to it.: no contrast between historical and modern reactions is made anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — To present the argument that paintings by the Florida Highwaymen likely helped to create a particular widespread impression of Florida.: this matches the passage's central claim — Highwaymen paintings played a role in shaping popular perceptions of Florida, with their natural iconography now seen as classically Floridian. ✓
That is the correct answer.
